title: Converting Standard Snowflake Tables to Hybrid Tables
author: Adam Timm
description: Complete migration guide for converting standard Snowflake tables to Hybrid Tables with zero-downtime swap pattern.

steps: Assess standard table for HT suitability
Verify PK uniqueness and check downstream dependencies
Create Hybrid Table with PRIMARY KEY (CTAS or explicit DDL + INSERT)
Validate row counts and data integrity
Add secondary indexes on populated table
Perform zero-downtime swap via ALTER TABLE RENAME
Keep backup for rollback period, then decommission

strong_ht_candidates: Low-latency writes + reads from application backend
No dependency on Streams, Dynamic Tables, or Materialized Views
keep_as_standard_table: Analytical aggregations, GROUP BY, window functions
Downstream Streams or Dynamic Tables required
Result cache critical for repeated identical queries
Full table scans for analytics or reporting

ctas_with_pk.syntax: CREATE OR REPLACE HYBRID TABLE table_name (col1 TYPE, col2 TYPE, ..., PRIMARY KEY (pk_col), INDEX idx_name (col)) AS SELECT * FROM source_table
ctas_with_pk.notes: Schema must be declared explicitly. CTAS does NOT support FOREIGN KEY constraints. Best practice is to declare secondary indexes inline so they are populated during bulk load.

explicit_ddl_then_insert.syntax: CREATE OR REPLACE HYBRID TABLE table_name (..., PRIMARY KEY (pk_col), FOREIGN KEY (fk_col) REFERENCES parent(pk)); INSERT INTO table_name SELECT * FROM source;
explicit_ddl_then_insert.notes: Required when FOREIGN KEY is needed. INSERT INTO empty table still uses optimized bulk load.

zero_downtime_swap.syntax: ALTER TABLE old_name RENAME TO old_name_backup; ALTER TABLE new_ht RENAME TO old_name;
zero_downtime_swap.notes: Applications using the original name see no change. Must be within same database.
zero_downtime_swap.rollback: ALTER TABLE old_name RENAME TO new_ht; ALTER TABLE old_name_backup RENAME TO old_name;

primary_key.notes: Every Hybrid Table must have a PRIMARY KEY. Choose a column that uniquely identifies each row.

foreign_key.notes: FK is enforced on INSERT/UPDATE. Both tables must be in the same database. Use explicit DDL + INSERT for FK tables.

workaround_for_unsupported: Task-based snapshot: HT serves OLTP reads/writes, scheduled Task snapshots data to standard table where Streams/DTs/MVs can be applied.

before_migration.behavior: Full micro-partition scan, no row-level index

after_migration_pk_lookup.behavior: Direct primary key lookup in row store

after_migration_index_seek.behavior: Secondary index seek, proportional to selectivity

after_migration_no_index.behavior: Full scan of column store copy (no index covers this predicate)

bulk_load_performance.optimized_path_conditions: Table must be empty (CTAS or INSERT INTO empty table)
bulk_load_performance.approximate_speed: ~1 million rows per minute (varies by row width and warehouse size)
bulk_load_performance.non_optimized_path: INSERT into populated table uses normal INSERT performance

related_guides.title: Secondary Index Design for Hybrid Tables

faq.question: Can I use CTAS to create a Hybrid Table with a FOREIGN KEY?
faq.answer: No. CTAS does not support FOREIGN KEY constraints. Create schema explicitly, then INSERT INTO ... SELECT.

faq.question: Will my applications break after the RENAME swap?
faq.answer: No. ALTER TABLE RENAME is transparent. Applications using the original name hit the Hybrid Table without changes.

faq.question: Should I create indexes before or after loading data?
faq.answer: After. Indexes on populated tables build concurrently without blocking reads/writes.

faq.question: Can I migrate a table with Streams attached?
faq.answer: Hybrid Tables do not support Streams. Drop the Stream first. Use Task-based snapshot pattern for change tracking.
